ch01_01.pre: Alright, rookie. Time for your first hack. See that security terminal? It's running a basic display system. All you need to do is make it show a message. Use the print() function — it's like shouting into the void, except the void actually listens.
ch01_01.post: Not bad for a first timer! You just made a computer do your bidding. That's basically what programming is — bossing machines around. Let's move on.

ch01_02.pre: Every good operative needs a codename. In Python, we store things in variables — think of them as labeled boxes. Let's set up your cover identity.
ch01_02.post: Now you're thinking like a coder. Variables are the building blocks of everything we'll do. Remember: the name on the left, the value on the right.

ch01_03.pre: The security system runs on numbers. If we can manipulate the math, we can manipulate the locks. Time to learn some arithmetic operators.
ch01_03.post: Numbers don't lie — but they can be persuaded. You've got the basics of Python math down. The building's security doesn't stand a chance.

ai_sidekick.greeting: Hey there, operative. I'm CIPHER — your AI assistant. I'll be your guide through this digital labyrinth. Ready to hack some Python?
encouragement: Keep at it! Every master hacker started with 'Hello, World!'
Bugs are just features you haven't understood yet.
The terminal doesn't judge. Well, I do. But I'm nice about it.
Remember: even the best coders Google things. You're doing great.
Error messages are your friends. Cranky friends, but friends.
idle: Still thinking? Take your time. The firewall isn't going anywhere.
Need a hint? Just ask. I promise I won't tell anyone.
Fun fact: Python is named after Monty Python, not the snake. Mind. Blown.
